project.name: qq
project.description: surlej's bespoke agentic-dev command center — sharp skills, operating rules, a code-knowledge layer, parallel named agent sessions, and a tuned terminal cockpit, with only the external tools that earn their keep.

nodes.name: .mcp.json
nodes.summary: Model Context Protocol configuration that registers the Context7 MCP server (launched via npx) to give agents live, version-correct library documentation.

nodes.name: .no-mistakes.yaml
nodes.summary: Repository configuration for qq's blast-radius merge gate (the external MIT no-mistakes pipeline): selects the codex agent, wires shellcheck linting of the bin/ scripts, and enables intent validation with the evidence trail kept out of the working tree.
nodes.languageNotes: agent and commands are read from the default branch by a no-mistakes security rule, so the file only takes effect once committed to main.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Authoritative operating rules for the qq agent command center, loaded every session — defines the six layers, the behavioral floor, task routing, the Align-to-Compound loop, git landing rules, and the skill index.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Symlink to AGENTS.md so Claude Code loads the same operating rules; identical content covering qq's six layers, behavioral floor, task routing, the loop, git rules, and the skill index.
nodes.languageNotes: This file is a symbolic link to AGENTS.md, not a separate copy — edits to either are the same bytes.

nodes.name: CONCEPTS.md
nodes.summary: Durable project glossary of qq-specific domain vocabulary, appended by the ce-compound skill as concepts stabilize so agents share consistent language across sessions.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project overview and setup guide for qq — surlej's bespoke agentic-dev command center — mapping the six layers and the loop and walking through preflight, activation, plugin install, cockpit, Context7, the knowledge layer, and session setup.

nodes.name: SKILLS-ATTRIBUTION.md
nodes.summary: Attribution and licensing record for qq's skill set, listing each vendored MIT skill with its upstream source and author, plus the four skills authored for qq and what each synthesizes.

nodes.name: install.sh
nodes.summary: Preflight checker for qq's external surface — verifies the fast-filesystem tools (ripgrep/fd/eza/gh), herdr, the cockpit tools (yazi/broot/glow/mdcat), the knowledge graph, the Context7 MCP config, and vendored skills are present, printing exact per-platform install hints. Inspects and instructs only; it never installs packages or uses sudo.
nodes.languageNotes: Idempotent bash that probes availability with `command -v` and emits brew/apt install hints via a small pkg_hint helper; strictly read-only against the system.

nodes.name: qq-activate.sh
nodes.summary: One-shot, idempotent activation of the qq setup: installs herdr agent-state integrations, symlinks the tuned cockpit configs into ~/.config, installs the git-guardrail + WIP-savepoint hooks, enables Claude Code and Codex yolo modes, and commits/pushes the qq layer to both the qq repo and a sibling meeting-reviewer repo. Backs up every file it edits to *.qq.bak.
nodes.languageNotes: Embeds a python3 heredoc to idempotently merge JSON into ~/.claude/settings.json (wiring the PreToolUse rail and Stop savepoint), and uses grep/sed to upsert top-level TOML keys in ~/.codex/config.toml; installs the rail before enabling yolo.

nodes.name: qq-wip-snapshot.sh
nodes.summary: Claude Code Stop-hook that non-destructively snapshots the working tree to refs/wip/<branch>, capturing in-flight work between green commits without touching HEAD, the index, or any working file. No-ops when outside a repo, when the tree is clean, or when nothing changed since the last snapshot.
nodes.languageNotes: Builds the snapshot out-of-band via a temporary GIT_INDEX_FILE with git read-tree/add/write-tree/commit-tree, then moves refs/wip/<branch> with update-ref — writing only new objects and refs so normal git operations are unaffected.

nodes.name: 2026-07-06-no-mistakes-gate-integration.md
nodes.summary: Five-task implementation plan for integrating the external no-mistakes gate into the repo: a GO/NO-GO trial in a scratch clone, installing and configuring the gate on the real repo, rewriting AGENTS.md around the merge gate, dogfooding one real change to a PR, and compounding the decision.
nodes.languageNotes: Structured implementation plan with per-task acceptance gates; references the repo's earlier hypercore identity prior to the qq rename.

nodes.name: 2026-07-06-no-mistakes-trial-report.md
nodes.summary: GO/NO-GO trial report for the no-mistakes gate, recording a GO verdict with evidence from a harmless trial change run end-to-end through the pipeline, including init-manifest contents, lint auto-fix proof, cost/latency figures, and safety confirmations.
nodes.languageNotes: Evidence-first trial report supporting the gate-integration plan's Task 1 decision gate.

nodes.name: 2026-07-06-qq-ac-reframe.md
nodes.summary: Four-phase implementation plan that reframes the project (rename hypercore to qq-ac): vendoring cockpit configs, renaming wip bin scripts, rewriting the activation script to symlink the cockpit, renaming the plugin identity, reframing the narrative docs, then verifying, landing through the gate, and flipping GitHub plus the on-disk directory.
nodes.languageNotes: Historical rename record; the qq-ac/hypercore names in the body are intentional, documenting the very rename this plan enacts.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Explains the purpose of the ideas/ backlog folder — an informal, no-ceremony holding pen for not-yet-built proposals — and indexes current backlog entries with one-line pointers to their detailed write-ups.

nodes.name: 01-btw-ideas-skill.md
nodes.summary: Design write-up for a proposed /btw ideas-capture skill that instantly banks a mid-session thought, sharpens it against session context, and researches it in the background; records the sharpened design, its relationship to the handoff skill, and three open decisions with recommendations.

nodes.name: 02-orchestrate-phase-state.md
nodes.summary: Notes on a built-but-unmerged orchestrate progress-state feature (qq-phase stamping loop phase to .orchestrate/state.json), salvaged as a re-appliable patch from a pruned branch, with instructions to resurrect it and caveats about expected conflicts.

nodes.name: marketplace.json
nodes.summary: Claude Code marketplace manifest that publishes qq as a single-plugin marketplace owned by surlej, pointing the qq plugin at the repo root.

nodes.name: plugin.json
nodes.summary: Claude Code plugin manifest declaring the qq plugin (name, version 0.1.0, MIT license) that bundles qq's curated skills, rules, knowledge, session, and cockpit layers.

nodes.name: qq-wip
nodes.summary: WIP-recovery CLI that lists, diffs, and materializes the non-destructive working-tree savepoints qq snapshots to refs/wip/<branch>, so lost in-flight work can be recovered onto a fresh branch.
nodes.languageNotes: POSIX bash with set -euo pipefail and a case dispatch over the list/diff/branch subcommands.

nodes.name: glow.yml
nodes.summary: Glow defaults for in-pane markdown rendering — fixed 80-column width, no pager, no mouse — used when files open inside cockpit terminal panes.

nodes.name: tuned.json
nodes.summary: Hand-tuned Glow markdown theme defining colors and styling for every element (headings, code, tables, links) rendered in cockpit panes.

nodes.name: config.toml
nodes.summary: Herdr multiplexer config: tokyo-night theme, agent sorting, and cockpit pane bindings (prefix+f runs qqy, prefix+shift+f runs qqbr).

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Documents the cockpit terminal surface — the yazi/glow/herdr configs and shell navigation helpers symlinked into ~/.config — and the pane-navigation flow between them.

nodes.name: file-navigation.bash
nodes.summary: Terminal navigation helpers for herdr panes and shells: generic yazi/broot wrappers (y, br) that cd to the chosen directory, plus repo-focused qqroot/qqy/qqbr and qfiles/qtree aliases.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses cwd-file / --outcmd handoff so yazi and broot can change the parent shell's directory.

nodes.name: keymap.toml
nodes.summary: Yazi keymap overrides: `!` opens a shell in the current directory and `g H` jumps to the qq project root.

nodes.name: yazi.toml
nodes.summary: Yazi file-manager config for pane-first navigation, opening markdown in-pane via mdcat with tuned Glow as the alternate opener.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: README for the docs/solutions/ directory where the ce-compound skill records durable solution write-ups for solved problems.

nodes.name: 02-orchestrate-phase-state.patch
nodes.summary: Proposed git patch adding bin/qq-phase as the single writer of loop-phase state to .orchestrate/state.json and wiring the orchestrate skill to stamp each phase boundary for a token-free progress widget.
nodes.languageNotes: Unified git-format-patch diff touching .gitignore, .no-mistakes.yaml, AGENTS.md, bin/qq-phase, and skills/orchestrate/SKILL.md.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Defines the ce-compound skill: after a verified fix, capture a solution doc under docs/solutions/ and record durable vocabulary in CONCEPTS.md so the next session doesn't relearn it.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Defines the code-review skill: review changes since a fixed point along two axes — Standards and Spec — via parallel sub-agents, reporting findings side by side.

nodes.name: hitl-loop.template.sh
nodes.summary: Copyable human-in-the-loop template: step() prompts the user through reproduction actions and capture() reads their answers into variables printed as KEY=VALUE for the agent to parse.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Defines the diagnosing-bugs skill: a six-phase loop (feedback loop, reproduce/minimise, hypothesise, instrument, fix + regression test, cleanup) for hard bugs and performance regressions.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Defines the executing-plans skill: work a written implementation plan task-by-task with review checkpoints, stopping on blockers and never touching main without consent.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Defines the finishing-a-development-branch skill: verify tests, detect environment and base branch, then present structured merge / PR / keep / discard options and clean up the workspace.

nodes.name: block-dangerous-git.sh
nodes.summary: Claude Code PreToolUse(Bash) hook that allows normal git push but blocks destructive operations — force-push, reset --hard, clean -f, branch -D, checkout/restore ., and history rewrites — by pattern-matching the command.
nodes.languageNotes: Reads tool_input.command via jq and greps it against a DANGEROUS_PATTERNS array, exiting 2 to block.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Defines the git-guardrails skill: install a Claude Code hook that blocks dangerous git commands before they execute, covering setup scope, hook wiring, and verification.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Thin alias skill that simply runs a /grilling session; a user-invocable entry point (disable-model-invocation) for the grilling interview.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Defines the grilling skill: a relentless one-question-at-a-time interview that walks the design tree and resolves decisions before any building begins.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Defines the handoff skill: compact the current session state into a briefing so a fresh agent can resume work when the context window fills.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Defines the orchestrate skill: conduct a non-trivial task through qq's full loop as a two-model split — Claude conducts and judges, Codex implements — from align through verify, review, and compound.

nodes.name: y
nodes.summary: Runs yazi with a temporary cwd-file, then cds the parent shell to the directory the user selected on exit.

nodes.name: br
nodes.summary: Runs broot with --outcmd and evals the emitted command so broot can change the parent shell's directory.

nodes.name: qqroot
nodes.summary: Cds to the qq project root ($QQ_HOME), printing an error and returning non-zero if it does not exist.

nodes.name: qqy
nodes.summary: Opens yazi (via y) rooted at the qq project when $QQ_HOME exists, otherwise at the current directory.

nodes.name: qqbr
nodes.summary: Opens broot (via br) rooted at the qq project when $QQ_HOME exists, otherwise at the current directory.

nodes.name: receiving-code-review/SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Defines the qq 'receiving-code-review' skill: a response protocol for weighing code-review feedback with technical rigor and verification rather than performative agreement or blind implementation. Covers forbidden responses, when to push back, and how to handle unclear or external-reviewer feedback.
nodes.languageNotes: Skill markdown with 17 sections; frontmatter description drives Claude Code's skill dispatcher.

nodes.name: research/SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Defines the qq 'research' skill: investigates a question against primary sources and Context7, adversarially verifies each claim, and leaves one cited, confidence-tagged Markdown file in research/. A compact skill covering method, output format, and when not to use it.

nodes.name: uat-signoff/SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Defines the qq 'uat-signoff' skill: a human acceptance sign-off that walks the owner through one observable test at a time to confirm a change is what they actually wanted, inferring severity for any gaps. Documents when it fires, its precondition on verification evidence, and how to run the checkpoint.

nodes.name: verification-before-completion/SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Defines the qq 'verification-before-completion' skill: the never-skipped gate requiring real verification commands and confirmed output before any 'done/fixed/passing' claim. States the Iron Law, a gate function, common failures, and rationalization-prevention guidance.
nodes.languageNotes: Central invariant skill referenced across the qq loop; enforces evidence-before-assertion.

nodes.name: writing-plans/plan-document-reviewer-prompt.md
nodes.summary: Prompt template used by the 'writing-plans' skill to review a drafted plan document before execution. A single-section helper asset rather than a standalone skill definition.

nodes.name: writing-plans/SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Defines the qq 'writing-plans' skill: turns a spec or requirements for a multi-step task into an executable, bite-sized plan document before touching code. Covers scope check, file structure, task right-sizing, plan headers, self-review, and execution handoff.

nodes.name: writing-skills/SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Defines the qq 'writing-skills' meta-skill: an eval-first, RED/GREEN/REFACTOR process for authoring or editing qq skills, including matching degrees of freedom to the task, countering rationalizations, structural passes, and an optional judge layer.

layers.name: Rules
layers.description: The behavioral floor and navigation backbone loaded every session — the operating rules (AGENTS.md and its CLAUDE.md symlink), project overview (README.md), the durable glossary (CONCEPTS.md), and the skill-provenance record (SKILLS-ATTRIBUTION.md).

layers.name: Actions (Skills)
layers.description: qq's atomic capabilities under skills/ — one SKILL.md per skill (grilling, orchestrate, code-review, verification, git-guardrails, and the rest) plus their bundled helper prompts and shell scripts, invoked by name from the loop.

layers.name: Cockpit
layers.description: The tuned terminal surface under cockpit/ — herdr/yazi/glow configs, the shell navigation helpers symlinked into ~/.config, and the README documenting the pane-navigation flow between them.

layers.name: Externals & Plugin Surface
layers.description: The thin outward-facing surface — the Context7 MCP registration (.mcp.json), the no-mistakes merge-gate config (.no-mistakes.yaml), and the Claude Code plugin/marketplace manifests that publish qq as a plugin.

layers.name: Setup & Bootstrap
layers.description: The bin/ scripts that stand qq up and keep it safe — the preflight checker, the idempotent activation script that symlinks the cockpit and installs hooks, and the WIP savepoint snapshot hook plus its recovery CLI.

layers.name: Planning & History
layers.description: The narrative record of decisions and pending work — the docs/ implementation plans and solution history plus the ideas/ backlog of not-yet-built proposals and its salvaged patch.

tour.title: What is qq?
tour.description: Start here. README.md is the map and the setup guide for qq — surlej's bespoke agentic-dev command center, packaged as a Claude Code plugin. Its guiding line is "capability I reach for, not process I maintain": qq is a curated set of skills, rules, a knowledge layer, parallel sessions, and a tuned terminal cockpit that earn their place by being invoked, not reported to. The README lays out the six-layer model and the core build loop that the rest of this tour walks through in order.

tour.title: The Operating Rules
tour.description: AGENTS.md is the behavioral floor loaded into every session — the single highest-connected file in the repo. It defines the six layers, the routing rules that triage each task by size and reversibility, the Align-to-Compound loop, how git work lands, and the skill index that names every capability. CLAUDE.md is a symlink to the same bytes so Claude Code and any AGENTS-aware tool read identical rules, and CONCEPTS.md is the durable glossary that keeps vocabulary consistent across sessions.
tour.languageLesson: A symlink (CLAUDE.md → AGENTS.md) is one file under two names — editing either changes the same bytes on disk. qq uses it so two different tool conventions (Claude Code expects CLAUDE.md, the wider ecosystem expects AGENTS.md) share one source of truth instead of drifting apart.

tour.title: Aligning Before Building
tour.description: Now we enter the Actions layer under skills/ — atomic capabilities the rules invoke by name. The loop opens with alignment: grilling runs a relentless one-question-at-a-time interview that walks the design tree and settles decisions before any code is written, and grill-me is the thin user-invocable alias that fires it. Once intent is pinned, writing-plans turns it into a bite-sized, executable plan document — the artifact the next steps consume.
tour.languageLesson: Each skill is a SKILL.md whose YAML frontmatter description drives Claude Code's skill dispatcher — that sentence is what decides whether the model reaches for the skill, so it is written as a precise trigger, not a summary.

tour.title: Orchestrate: Running the Whole Loop
tour.description: orchestrate is the marquee skill — it conducts a non-trivial task through qq's entire loop as a two-model split: Claude aligns, plans, verifies, and judges while Codex does the implementation, so the conductor's context stays clean and the reviewer is never the author. It leans on executing-plans to work a plan task-by-task with review checkpoints (never touching main without consent), and on finishing-a-development-branch to land the result via merge, PR, or the gate. Together these three are the spine of how real work moves from intent to a landed change.

tour.title: Verify, Review, Sign Off
tour.description: These skills are the quality gates the loop refuses to skip. verification-before-completion is the never-skipped invariant: no "done/fixed/passing" claim without running the real command and reading its output — evidence before assertions. code-review audits a diff along Standards and Spec axes via parallel sub-agents, receiving-code-review governs how feedback is weighed with rigor rather than rubber-stamped, and uat-signoff walks the human owner through one observable test at a time for user-facing or irreversible changes.

tour.title: Diagnose, Compound, and Support
tour.description: The remaining skills support the loop from the edges. diagnosing-bugs is a six-phase loop for hard bugs and performance regressions; ce-compound captures a just-solved problem so the next session doesn't relearn it (it is what appends to CONCEPTS.md from Step 2); handoff compacts session state when the context window fills; research investigates a question against cited primary sources; and writing-skills is the eval-first meta-skill for authoring new skills. This closes the Actions layer — the full vocabulary of what qq can do.

tour.title: The Cockpit
tour.description: The cockpit/ directory is the human-driven terminal surface, symlinked into ~/.config so the repo stays the live source of truth. Its README documents the pane-navigation flow between the tools: file-navigation.bash is the hub of shell helpers (y/br wrappers plus repo-focused qqroot/qqy/qqbr), herdr/config.toml sets the tokyo-night multiplexer theme and binds prefix+f to those helpers, yazi.toml drives pane-first file browsing that opens markdown in place, and glow/tuned.json is the hand-tuned markdown theme those panes render with.
tour.languageLesson: The shell wrappers use a cwd-file / --outcmd handoff: a child tool (yazi, broot) writes the directory the user picked to a temp file, and the parent shell reads it back to cd there — a subprocess normally can't change its parent's working directory, so this indirection is the workaround.

tour.title: Externals & Plugin Surface
tour.description: This is qq's thin outward-facing surface — how it reaches external tools and how it publishes itself. .mcp.json registers the Context7 MCP server that gives agents live, version-correct library docs; .no-mistakes.yaml configures the external blast-radius merge gate (selecting Codex, wiring shellcheck on bin/, enabling intent validation). plugin.json and marketplace.json declare qq as an installable Claude Code plugin bundling all six layers, and SKILLS-ATTRIBUTION.md records the MIT provenance of every vendored skill plus the four authored for qq.
tour.languageLesson: The .no-mistakes.yaml agent and commands are read from the default branch by a security rule, so the config only takes effect once committed to main — a deliberate safeguard so a feature branch can't reconfigure the gate that reviews it.

tour.title: Bootstrap: Preflight & Activation
tour.description: The bin/ scripts stand qq up on a new machine. install.sh is a read-only preflight checker: it probes for the fast-filesystem tools, herdr, and the cockpit binaries and prints exact per-platform install hints, but never installs anything or uses sudo. qq-activate.sh is the idempotent one-shot that actually wires things up — symlinking the cockpit configs from Step 8 into ~/.config, installing the safety hooks from the next step, enabling yolo modes, and backing up every file it edits to *.qq.bak.
tour.languageLesson: Both scripts are idempotent bash — running them twice is safe and produces the same result. Activation embeds a python3 heredoc to merge JSON into ~/.claude/settings.json rather than overwrite it, so it can wire in hooks without clobbering the operator's existing settings.

tour.title: Safety Net: The Git Rail & WIP Savepoints
tour.description: Activation installs two always-on protections. The git-guardrails skill and its block-dangerous-git.sh hook run as a Claude Code PreToolUse gate that allows normal git push but blocks force-push, reset --hard, clean -f, branch -D, and history rewrites before they execute. qq-wip-snapshot.sh is the complementary Stop-hook that non-destructively snapshots the working tree to refs/wip/<branch> on every idle, and qq-wip is the recovery CLI that lists, diffs, and materializes those savepoints onto a fresh branch — so in-flight work between green commits is never lost.
tour.languageLesson: The savepoint hook builds its snapshot out-of-band via a temporary GIT_INDEX_FILE with read-tree/write-tree/commit-tree, writing only new git objects and a wip ref — it never touches HEAD, the index, or any working file, so normal git operations are completely unaffected.

tour.title: Planning & History
tour.description: The last layer is qq's narrative memory — the record of how it got here and what's next. The qq-ac-reframe plan documents the full hypercore → qq-ac → qq rebuild that produced the current identity, while the no-mistakes gate-integration plan and its GO/NO-GO trial report capture how the merge gate was trialed and adopted with evidence. docs/solutions/README.md is where ce-compound (Step 6) files durable solution write-ups, and ideas/README.md indexes the informal backlog of not-yet-built proposals. Reading these closes the loop: qq compounds its own decisions so the next session starts ahead.
